compiled_state.author: Iso Tester

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
Implemented on branch feat/parent-first-claim-cascade (commit 9891b1e2).

- _resolve_open_parent helper (transition.py) returns the parent id only when the parent exists and is open.
- claim_compute (claim.py) and transition_compute (transition.py, guarded to the open->in_progress edge) cascade to the open parent first, recursively up the chain, cycle-guarded via a _cascade_seen frozenset. Same assignee carried up on claim. Parent failure aborts the child and re-raises a CommandError naming the parent, preserving the parent's exit code; claim_cli now returns exc.returncode (so a parent concurrency conflict stays exit 10).
- Docs: CLAUDE.md (workflow step 2 + new 'Parent-first claim/transition cascade' subsection) and docs/concurrency.md (invariant I4a). CLI --help usage strings updated for both commands.
- Tests: tests/interfaces/lifecycle/test_claim_transition_parent_cascade.py (11 tests) all pass; lifecycle+facades suites green (only pre-existing live-Jira reconcile test fails, unrelated/environmental); claim-storm concurrency tests pass; CLI smoke test confirmed a leaf claim cascades epic+story+task to in_progress for the same assignee.

compiled_state.description:
## What
Adjust the claim/transition-to-in_progress flow so that grabbing a child first grabs its open parent.

When a client `claim`s a ticket, or `transition`s a ticket from `open` to `in_progress`, evaluate whether the ticket has a parent. If the parent exists **and is in `open` status** (not already in_progress/closed/blocked), attempt the same claim/transition operation on the parent **before** running it on the child. This cascades up the hierarchy (grandparent first).

If the operation fails on the parent, the operation is **not** attempted on the child, and the error message must clearly inform the caller that the failure was on the **parent** ticket (not the requested child).

## Scope
- `src/rebar/_commands/claim.py` (`claim_compute`)
- `src/rebar/_commands/transition.py` (`transition_compute`)
- Document the cascade in client-facing docs: `CLAUDE.md`, `docs/concurrency.md`.
- Tests under `tests/interfaces/lifecycle/`.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Claiming a child whose parent is `open` claims the parent first (and recursively up the chain), then the child.
- [ ] Transitioning a child `open`->`in_progress` whose parent is `open` transitions the parent first, then the child.
- [ ] A parent already `in_progress`/`closed`/`blocked` is NOT cascaded; the child operation proceeds alone.
- [ ] If the parent operation fails, the child operation is NOT attempted and the error message names the parent as the cause.
- [ ] Cycle-safe (no infinite recursion on a malformed parent cycle).
- [ ] Other transitions (close, reopen, etc.) are unaffected.
- [ ] Behavior documented in CLAUDE.md and docs/concurrency.md.
- [ ] Tests cover: happy cascade, multi-level cascade, parent-not-open no-op, parent-failure aborts child with attributed error.
compiled_state.title: Parent-first cascade for claim/transition (open->in_progress)
